---
title: "01_Data processing"
authors: "Trudy, Jenny, Janice, ZZ"
output: html_document
date: "2026-04-04"
---

```{r setup}

library(tidyverse)
library(rvest)
library(here)
library(lubridate)
library(trend)
library(cowplot)

```


## Data Input
```{r}
price <- read_excel("Data/Cushing, OK WTI Spot Price FOB (Dollars per Barrel).xls")
stock <- read_excel("Data/U.S. Ending Stocks of Crude Oil (Thousand Barrels).xls")
production <- read_excel("Data/U.S. Field Production of Crude Oil (Thousand Barrels per Day).xls")
```

## Including Plots

You can also embed plots, for example:

```{r pressure, echo=FALSE}
plot(pressure)
```

Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.
